notes: Oneironautics' 2005 course-correct after Estuary 3's cult reception.  Bigger budget, more species, a proper narrative campaign.  ~14 hours.  The hunter's cabin in Pirate Summer's east forest is this game's breadcrumb.

shelf.shelf_condition: near mint · Tem's grandfather bought it and never opened the manual
shelf.provenance_note: Bought the week of release out of loyalty.  He was an Estuary 1 person.  He said 4 was 'too much game.'  He meant it kindly and never played past the second hour.
shelf.in_fiction_dev_note: The campaign was Ines Rocha's pitch, greenlit after Estuary 3's slow-burn word of mouth finally reached the trade press.  Twice the team, half the weirdness · the reviews were better and the letters were fewer.  Ines kept every letter Estuary 3 got and none of Estuary 4's.

acts_deferred_note: Playable content authoring deferred.  Design intent: four-season campaign, fourteen hours, a returning-species memory system that recognizes your Estuary 3 save.
